name: Keystone: Mitochondria Signaling in Physiology and Disease 2026
desc: Keystone Symposium on mitochondria as signaling organelles that control cellular and organismal physiology, and how mitochondrial dysfunction drives disease.
mtor: TIER 3 — Mitochondrial metabolism and nutrient status are tightly integrated with mTORC1 signaling; mitochondrial cues feed into growth control central to the Atlas. Downgraded to Tier 3: mTOR is not a named topic anywhere in the program — the connection is inferred adjacency (mitochondrial cues feeding nutrient sensing), not stated content. Retained for completeness, not prioritised.
topics: Mitochondrial signaling; metabolites as signals; redox; mitochondria in immunity, cancer and aging.
organizers: Scientific organizers: Navdeep S. Chandel; Aleksandra Trifunovic.
speakers: Organizers: Navdeep S. Chandel, Aleksandra Trifunovic. Keynote: Anu Suomalainen Wartiovaara. Invited/session speakers: Elena Ziviani, Kivanc Birsoy, Dipayan Chaudhuri, Dustin Hancks, Xin Jie Chen, Phillip West, María Mittelbrunn, Lena Pernas, Michelle Clasquin, Andrew G. Dillin, Jose Antonio Enríquez, Peter M. Douglas, Dave Pagliarini, Prashant Mishra, Reuben J. Shaw, Jan Riemer, Marcia C. Haigis, Jay H. Chung, Janine Santos, Liron Bar-Peled, Mike P. Murphy.

name: 7th European Meeting on AMPK & Nutrient-Sensing Pathways 2026
desc: Triennial European meeting on AMPK and nutrient-sensing signalling. Published program includes dedicated sessions.
mtor: TIER 2 — AMPK is the principal upstream antagonist of mTORC1.
topics: AMPK and AMPK-related kinase signalling; nutrient sensing beyond AMPK (incl. mTOR); AMPK in exercise and cardiometabolic disease; immunometabolism; AMPK family in cancer, aging and longevity.
organizers: European AMPK community
speakers: Full speaker roster not yet resolved against the Atlas Authors table; Linked_Authors left blank until confirmed. Grahame Hardie (AMPK) and Reuben Shaw (LKB1-AMPK-mTOR, Atlas author) are the field-defining figures this series typically features.

name: Gordon Research Conference: Systems Aging 2026
desc: Gordon Research Conference on Systems Aging.
mtor: TIER 2 — Nutrient-sensing and mTOR / insulin-IGF signaling are central pillars of systems-level aging biology.
topics: Evolutionary perspectives on aging; invertebrate models; vertebrate models; genome; biomarkers and quantitative aging; frailty; systems aging and immunology; reproductive aging; human longevity.
organizers: Conference chair(s) not yet publicly listed as of July 2026.
speakers: Speaker list not yet published as of July 2026.

name: CSHL: Mechanisms of Aging 2026
desc: CSHL meeting on the regulation and function of genes and processes known or suspected to control cellular and organismal aging.
mtor: TIER 2 — Nutrient-sensing longevity pathways (TOR/AMPK/insulin-IGF) are central.
topics: Aging mechanisms; nutrient sensing (TOR/AMPK); brain aging; cellular biology; longevity interventions.
organizers: Organizers: Rozalyn Anderson; William Mair; Ashley Webb.
speakers: Keynote: Rafael de Cabo (NIA). Confirmed invited speakers: Joseph Baur, Andreas Beyer, Jens Brüning, Weiwei Dang, Jennifer Elisseeff, Judith Frydman, Vadim Gladyshev, Margaret Goodell, Catherine Kaczorowski, Caroline Kumsta, Dudley Lamming, Jodi Nunnari, Courtney Marie Peterson, Marissa Schafer, Ming Xu. Organizers: Rozalyn Anderson, William Mair, Ashley Webb.

name: AACR Annual Meeting 2027
desc: The largest cancer research meeting in the world.
mtor: TIER 2 — The principal venue where human data on mTOR-pathway inhibitors surface first.
topics: PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway in cancer; rapalog and next-generation mTOR inhibitor pharmacology; resistance mechanisms; tumour metabolism; clinical trial readouts.
organizers: American Association for Cancer Research (AACR)
speakers: Program not yet published.

name: 13th CFATG Scientific Days on Autophagy (Paris) 2026
desc: Annual meeting of the French Association for Autophagy Research (CFATG).
mtor: Features keynote Roberto Zoncu (UC Berkeley — leading mTOR/lysosome researcher and mTOR-autophagy axis expert), Sharon Tooze (Crick Institute, autophagy initiation upstream of mTOR), and Ian Ganley (mitophagy). Direct mechanistic overlap with Atlas autophagy-mTOR studies.
topics: Autophagy mechanisms; selective autophagy; lysosomal biology; immunity; cancer; aging
organizers: CFATG (Guillaume Beauclair, Aurore Claude-Taupin, Audrey Esclatine)
speakers: Ian Ganley, Carine Joffre, Louis Lapierre, Katja Simon, Sharon Tooze, Roberto Zoncu

name: Keystone: Autophagy — Mechanisms to Therapy 2026
desc: Keystone Symposium covering the roles and mechanisms of autophagy across health and disease.
mtor: TIER 2 — mTORC1 is the master negative regulator of autophagy; sessions address mTOR inhibition and nutrient control of autophagic flux.
topics: Autophagy machinery; selective autophagy; mTOR inhibition; lysosomal biology; autophagy in cancer and neurodegeneration; therapeutics.
organizers: Scientific organizers: Anne Simonsen; Sharon A. Tooze; Dan Baird.
speakers: Organizers: Anne Simonsen, Sharon A. Tooze, Dan Baird. Keynotes: Erika L. Holzbaur, Harald Stenmark. Invited/session speakers: Felix Randow, Oliver Florey, Jayanta Debnath, Rupert Beale, Maho Hamasaki, Zvulun Elazar, J. Wade Harper, Phyllis Hanson, James H. Hurley, Claudio Bussi, Ivan Dikic, Ai Yamamoto, Joseph D. Mancias, Anastasia Henry, Chris Shoemaker, Simon Wilkinson, Michael Lazarou, Chantell S. Evans, Martin Graef.

name: Keystone Symposia: Autophagy in Health and Disease — From Mechanisms to Therapeutics 2027
desc: Keystone Symposium covering core autophagy machinery, selective autophagy, non-canonical autophagy pathways, and therapeutic exploitation.
mtor: TIER 2 — mTORC1 is the principal negative regulator of autophagy initiation.
topics: Autophagosome biogenesis; selective autophagy; lysosomal signaling; non-canonical autophagy; autophagy in aging and neurodegeneration; therapeutic autophagy modulation.
organizers: Scientific Organizers: Hong Zhang, Susan Ferro-Novick, Hitoshi Nakatogawa.
speakers: Keynote: Noboru Mizushima. Invited: Hitoshi Nakatogawa, Sascha Martens, Nobuo N. Noda, Susan Ferro-Novick, Florian Wilfling, Eric H. Baehrecke, Ana Maria Cuervo, Hong Zhang, Xiaochen Wang, Roberto Zoncu, Vojo Deretic, Yan Zhao, Mondira Kundu, Dimitri Krainc, Patricia Boya, Ai Yamamoto, Anna Katharina Simon, Boxun Lu, Vassiliki Nikoletopoulou.

name: American Transplant Congress (ATC) 2027
desc: Joint annual meeting of AST and ASTS.
mtor: TIER 2 — Transplantation is the largest human exposure dataset for sirolimus and everolimus.
topics: mTOR inhibitors as immunosuppressants; sirolimus/everolimus protocols; long-term safety and metabolic toxicity.
organizers: American Society of Transplantation (AST) and American Society of Transplant Surgeons (ASTS)
speakers: Program not yet published.

name: ASCO Annual Meeting 2027
desc: The largest clinical oncology meeting worldwide.
mtor: TIER 2 — Where mTOR inhibition meets clinical endpoints in humans.
topics: Everolimus and temsirolimus clinical trials; bi-steric mTORC1-selective inhibitors; combination regimens.
organizers: American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO)
speakers: Program not yet published.

name: 2026 TSC World Conference
desc: TSC Alliance marquee event combining scientific and family tracks.
mtor: TIER 1 — TSC is the genetic disease that established the TSC1/TSC2->Rheb->mTORC1 axis, and the only indication where everolimus is approved on a directly genetic mTOR rationale.
topics: TSC genetics and mTORC1 hyperactivation; everolimus in SEGA, renal angiomyolipoma and refractory epilepsy.
organizers: TSC Alliance, co-hosted with TSCi
speakers: Speaker list not yet resolved against the Atlas Authors table.

name: International LAM Research Conference & LAMposium 2025
venue: Westin Kansas City at Crown Center
desc: The LAM Foundation's marquee event combining scientific and patient conference.
mtor: TIER 1 — LAM is the second approved mTOR-inhibitor indication (sirolimus, via the MILES trial).
topics: LAM cell biology and TSC2 loss; sirolimus efficacy and long-term outcomes; VEGF-D biomarker.
organizers: The LAM Foundation
speakers: Speaker list not resolved against the Atlas Authors table.

name: AACR-NCI-EORTC International Conference on Molecular Targets and Cancer Therapeutics 2027
desc: Small, drug-development-focused conference where first-in-human data on novel targeted agents are reported.
mtor: TIER 2 — Direct provenance for the Atlas's own Schram 2025 entry: first-in-human data for RMC-5552, the first bi-steric mTORC1-selective inhibitor in the clinic.
topics: Third-generation mTOR inhibitors; 4E-BP1 pharmacodynamics; PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway drugs.
organizers: AACR, NCI, EORTC
speakers: 2027 program not yet published.

name: Cell Symposia: Hallmarks of Aging 2026
desc: Cell Press Symposium exploring the cellular and molecular hallmarks of aging.
mtor: TIER 2 — Deregulated nutrient sensing (mTOR, AMPK, IIS) is a canonical hallmark of aging.
topics: Hallmarks of aging; nutrient sensing; autophagy; senescence; gerotherapeutics; metabolism.
organizers: Organizers: Rafael de Cabo (NIA); Ana Maria Cuervo (Albert Einstein); with Cell Press editors.
speakers: Keynote Luigi Ferrucci; confirmed speakers include Berenice Benayoun, Haim Cohen, Dolores Corella, Clara Correia-Melo, Vishwa Deep Dixit, David Glass, Mari-Carmen Gomez-Cabrera, among others.

name: AGE 54th Annual Meeting 2026 (American Aging Association)
desc: 54th Annual Meeting of the American Aging Association (AGE). Theme: Geroscience: From Discovery to Application.
mtor: TIER 2 — Flagship US geroscience meeting; nutrient-sensing / mTOR biology and rapamycin-class interventions are recurring core topics.
topics: Geroscience; nutrient-sensing and mTOR biology; caloric/dietary restriction mimetics; biomarkers of aging; sex differences in aging.
organizers: American Aging Association (AGE).
speakers: Program speakers/chairs incl. Steven Austad, Rafael de Cabo, Andrzej Bartke, Holly Brown-Borg, Alessandro Bitto, Vishwa Deep Dixit, Marianna Sadagurski, Michael Olivier, Michal Masternak, Liou Sun, Nathan Basisty, Stacy Hussong, Kevin Koronowski, Sarah Ocanas, Cristal Hill.

name: EMBO Workshop: Autophagy — Foundations, Functions and Physiology 2026
desc: EMBO Workshop focused on the latest advances in autophagy mechanism and selective autophagy.
mtor: mTOR is a primary regulator of autophagy induction; speakers including Roberto Zoncu (lysosome/mTOR) ensure direct Atlas relevance. Sessions cover autophagy–mTOR crosstalk in metabolism and disease.
topics: Selective autophagy mechanisms; ATG proteins; lysosomal biology; metabolism; immunity; cell differentiation
organizers: Fulvio Reggiori (Aarhus University)
speakers: Ian Ganley, Sharon Tooze, Roberto Zoncu, Katja Simon, Louis Lapierre, Carine Joffre

name: ARDD 2025 — Aging Research and Drug Discovery (12th)
desc: The 12th Aging Research and Drug Discovery meeting.
mtor: TIER 2 — Rapamycin/mTOR inhibition is one of the central pillars of aging pharmacology.
topics: Geroscience; drug discovery; senescence; rapamycin & mTOR; metabolic aging; clinical translation.
organizers: Organized by the University of Copenhagen (Scheibye-Knudsen group).
speakers: Speakers/participants included David Sinclair (Harvard), Marco Demaria, Kristen Fortney (BioAge), Morten Meldal (Nobel laureate), Andrew Adams (Eli Lilly), among many others.

name: Gordon Research Conference: Autophagy in Stress, Development and Disease 2026
desc: GRC on core autophagy machinery and its roles in cancer, aging, neurodegeneration.
mtor: TIER 2 — mTORC1 is the master negative regulator of autophagy; confirmed 2026 program includes a dedicated talk on the TFEB-mTORC1 Axis in Tumorigenesis.
topics: Core autophagy machinery; selective autophagy; autophagy in cancer and immunity; the TFEB-mTORC1 axis; mitophagy; lysosome membrane repair; autophagy and proteostasis.
organizers: Chairs and vice chairs not yet publicly named as of July 2026.

name: Prague Metabolism and Signaling Symposium 2026
venue: National Library of Technology (NTK), Campus Dejvice
desc: Meeting at the intersection of metabolism and signal transduction. ~30 invited speakers, chaired by David Sabatini and Katerina Rohlenova. A dedicated closing mTOR Biology session features Brendan Manning, Kuang Shen, Mario Pende and David Sabatini.
mtor: TIER 1 — Highest relevance in the calendar. Co-chaired by David Sabatini, keynote by Michael N. Hall (discovered TOR), and an explicit mTOR Biology session.
topics: Energy & metabolite sensing; organellar signaling; autophagy; aging; cancer, immune and stem-cell metabolism; host-pathogen metabolism; mTOR biology; structural biology.
organizers: Chairs: Katerina Rohlenova; David Sabatini. Keynotes: Karen Vousden; Michael N. Hall.
speakers: Keynotes: Karen Vousden, Michael N. Hall. Invited: Janelle Ayres, Liron Bar-Peled, Issam Ben Sahra, Kivanc Birsoy, Anne Brunet, Ralph DeBerardinis, Andrew Dillin, Alejo Efeyan, Mark Febbraio, Lydia Finley, Gerta Hoxhaj, Navdeep Chandel, Heather Christofk, Nada Kalaany, Naama Kanarek, Pekka Katajisto, Brendan Manning, Michael Pacold, Thales Papagiannakopoulos, Erika Pearce, Mario Pende, Richard Possemato, Jared Rutter, Luca Scorrano, Reuben Shaw, Kuang Shen, Celeste Simon, Jessica Spinelli, Eileen White, Omer Yilmaz.

name: Keystone Symposia: Healthy Aging & Therapeutic Opportunities 2027
desc: Keystone Symposia conference examining strategies for promoting healthy aging.
mtor: TIER 2 — Co-organized by Brian K. Kennedy, a leading rapamycin/mTOR and NAD+ longevity researcher.
topics: Geromedicine; biomarkers of aging; non-pharmacologic interventions; combination therapies; drug target identification.
organizers: Scientific Organizers: Derek Huffman; Kristen Fortney; Brian K. Kennedy; Shu-Chun Teng.
speakers: Confirmed speaker roster not yet published.

name: Fifth Geroscience Summit: Revisiting the Geroscience Hypothesis - Focus on Health
desc: NIA-hosted summit bringing together academic and industry researchers in geroscience.
mtor: TIER 2 — NIH/NIA flagship geroscience summit. Gerotherapeutic development and healthspan biomarker sessions directly track the Atlas's nutrient-sensing/mTOR-aging axis.
topics: Geroscience hypothesis; healthspan measurement; gerotherapeutics; biomarkers of aging; hallmarks of aging.
organizers: NIH/NIA Geroscience Interest Group (GSIG).
speakers: Keynote: Luigi Ferrucci. Session I: Juan Carlos Izpisua Belmonte, Thomas Rando, George Kuchel, Guanghui Liu. Session II: Jesse Poganik, Yousin Suh, Paola Sebastiani. Session III: Eileen Crimmins, Cathal McCrory, Jessica Faul, Dana Goldman, Andrew Scott. Session IV: Daniel Belsky, Jennifer Brach, Su-In Lee, Michael Snyder. Session V: John Newman, Heinrich Jasper, James Peyer, Brianna Stubbs. Session VI: Warren Casey, Kristen Brennand, Joseph Wu, Luiz Eduardo Bertassoni, Francesca Duncan. Session VII: Jamie Justice, Joan Mannick, Andrew Brack. Session VIII: TBD.

name: Keystone: Tumor Metabolism — New Frontiers in Diet, Microbiome and Beyond 2026
desc: Keystone Symposium exploring the reciprocal relationship between cancer and whole-body metabolism.
mtor: TIER 2 — The PI3K-AKT-mTOR axis is a central controller of tumor cell growth and metabolism.
topics: Tumor metabolism; diet and cancer; microbiome; nutrient sensing; therapeutic vulnerabilities.
organizers: Scientific organizers: Karen H. Vousden; Cynthia L. Sears; Eileen White.
speakers: Organizers: Karen H. Vousden, Cynthia L. Sears, Eileen White. Keynote: Heather Christofk. Invited/session speakers: Craig B. Thompson, Matthew G. name: 2026 Biomarkers of Aging Conference
venue: Joseph B. Martin Conference Center, Harvard Medical School
desc: Annual conference from the Biomarkers of Aging Consortium.
mtor: TIER 3 — Broad geroscience/biomarkers meeting. Downgraded to Tier 3: relevance rests on speaker affiliation rather than program content.
topics: Biomarkers of aging; longevity interventions; epigenetic clocks; multi-omics aging biomarkers; AI tools.
organizers: Conference Chairs: Vadim Gladyshev, Mahdi Moqri, Chiara Herzog, Andrea Cipriano, Jesse Poganik.

name: ARDD 2026 — Aging Research and Drug Discovery (13th)
desc: The 13th Aging Research & Drug Discovery (ARDD) meeting.
mtor: TIER 2 — Rapamycin/mTOR-based interventions are a core geroscience topic.
topics: Geroscience; drug discovery; senescence; rapamycin & mTOR; clinical translation.
organizers: Organized by Insilico Medicine. Chairs: Vadim Gladyshev; Jesse Poganik; Alex Zhavoronkov; Morten Scheibye-Knudsen; Daniela Bakula; Evelyne Bischof; Maximilian Unfried.
speakers: More speakers to be announced as of July 2026.

name: Keystone: Autophagosomes and Endolysosomes 2025
desc: Keystone Symposium on autophagosome and endolysosomal biogenesis and function.
mtor: TIER 2 — The lysosomal surface is the physical platform for mTORC1 activation (Rag GTPases, Ragulator).
topics: Autophagosome biogenesis; endolysosomal trafficking; lysosomal signaling; TFEB; disease implications.
organizers: Scientific organizers: Christina Towers; Rupert Beale; Leon O. Murphy.
speakers: Organizers/speakers: Christina Towers, Rupert Beale, Leon O. Murphy.
